caption:
This might be one of the most underrated wildlife destinations in the U.S. 🇺🇸

If you love nature, coastal views, and spotting animals in the wild, Point Reyes National Seashore is pure magic ✨

Here’s what you can see here 👇
🦌 Tule elk roaming freely at Tomales Point
🐘 Elephant seals resting along the beaches
🦅 Coastal birds, hawks, and endless marine life
🌊 Dramatic cliffs, foggy coastlines, and untouched landscapes

The best part? It’s just a short drive from San Francisco but it feels like a completely different world.

📌 Best tips for wildlife spotting:
• Go early morning or sunset (golden hour hits different here)
• Bring binoculars or zoom lens
• Stay quiet + keep distance (respect wildlife 🤍)
• Visit Tomales Point Trail for elk sightings

This is the kind of place you don’t just visit… you experience.

Would you add this to your California bucket list? 🫶🏻

caption:
Located at the base of the iconic Superstition Mountains, this is one of the best hiking spots near Phoenix, Arizona for desert views, golden hour photography, and beginner friendly trails.

Here are the best hikes in Lost Dutchman State Park ⬇️

✨ Siphon Draw Trail
– Most popular hike in the park
– Leads toward Flatiron (challenging at the top)
– Best for sunrise views + dramatic desert landscapes

✨ Treasure Loop Trail
– Easy to moderate loop (perfect for beginners)
– Classic Arizona desert views + saguaro cacti
– Great for sunset & casual hikes

✨ Jacob’s Crosscut Trail
– Scenic connector trail
– Less crowded + peaceful desert vibes
– Amazing for photography and wide mountain views

✨ Discovery Trail
– Short + easy nature walk
– Perfect if you want a quick hike with views
– Great for families or relaxed exploring

✨ Prospector’s View Trail
– Super short but rewarding
– Elevated view of the entire park
– One of the best quick photo spots 📸

💡 Best time to visit: October – April (avoid summer heat)
💡 Best time to hike: Sunrise or sunset for that golden Arizona glow

If you’re searching for:

caption:
You might still be able to see cherry blossoms in Chicago! 

📍 Where to See the Cherry Blossoms:
	•Location:
➔ Jackson Park, Chicago
➔ Near the Columbian Basin (by the Museum of Science and Industry)
➔ The main area is around Wooded Island and Columbian Basin Lagoon
➔ You can follow signs toward the Garden of the Phoenix (Japanese Garden), this area is stunning during bloom!

🚗 Where to Park:
	•Easiest Parking:
➔ Museum of Science and Industry Parking Garage (address: 5700 S Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60637)
➔ Then it’s a 5–10 minute walk to the cherry blossom spots near the Garden of the Phoenix and Wooded Island.
	•Street Parking:
➔ There’s limited free and metered parking around 59th Street, Midway Plaisance, and Lake Shore Drive, but it gets crowded on weekends!

•Tip:
➔ Go early morning (8–10 AM) for easier parking and fewer crowds and softer golden light for photos! ✨

•Bloom Timing:
➔ Peak bloom lasts about 5–7 days, so once they start, try to visit quickly! 🌸
➔ Watch weather forecasts windy/rainy days can knock petals off fast.

•Best Photo Spots:
➔ Along the edges of Columbian Basin Lagoon
➔ On the bridges around Wooded Island
➔ Inside the Garden of the Phoenix under the arching trees

🌸 About the Cherry Blossom Trees in Chicago (Jackson Park):
	•Chicago’s cherry blossom trees were originally a gift from Japan to symbolize friendship. 🇯🇵🤝🇺🇸
	•The trees in Jackson Park were planted near the Columbian Basin during the early 2010s to honor this relationship.
	•They don’t bloom every year because of Chicago’s harsh winters, the blossoms are sensitive and can be damaged.
	•The blooms typically peak in late April, but it’s very weather dependent, early warm spells can speed it up.

caption:
✨ 10 Unspoken Cultural Rules to Know Before Visiting Japan 🇯🇵
📌 SAVE THIS for your Japan itinerary, these small details will completely change your travel experience

Japan travel isn’t about being perfect…
it’s about being mindful, respectful, and aware of the culture around you 🤍

Once you understand these, everything from Tokyo trains to Kyoto temples, just flows effortlessly.

🌸 Observe first, follow gently (Japan cultural etiquette)
🚆 Trains are extremely punctual → always arrive early
🤍 Respect personal space (quiet, calm environments)
🍜 Slurping noodles = appreciation (Japanese food etiquette)
👟 Shoes off indoors (homes, temples, some restaurants)
📸 Be mindful with photos (especially in shrines, temples & geisha districts)
🧘‍♀️ Silence = respect (trains, buses, public spaces)
🙏 Temples are sacred spaces (not just photo spots)
💴 Cash is still widely used in Japan
🙇‍♀️ A small bow + smile goes a long way

These aren’t strict rules 
they’re the reason Japan feels so safe, peaceful, and beautifully organized ✨

If you’re planning a Japan trip, Tokyo itinerary, Kyoto travel guide, or first time visiting Japan, this is what most travelers don’t know… but should.

💬 Which one surprised you the most?

caption:
🚗 The Most Epic Coastal Road in Big Sur, California 🌊
This isn’t just a drive, it’s one of the most scenic road trips in the world.

Driving along Big Sur on Highway 1 feels unreal. Towering cliffs, endless Pacific Ocean views, and winding roads carved into the coastline make this stretch of California feel straight out of a movie.

From the iconic curves of Bixby Creek Bridge to the ocean side waterfall at McWay Falls, Big Sur is pure magic for road trip lovers, photographers, and nature seekers.

🌅 Best time to visit Big Sur: spring and fall for fewer crowds, coastal fog, and golden light
✈️ Closest airports: San Francisco (SFO), San Jose (SJC), Monterey (MRY)
🚘 Perfect for: California coastal road trip, weekend getaway, bucket list drive

📍 Don’t miss:
• Pfeiffer Beach – purple sand + sunset
• Garrapata Bluff – dramatic cliff views
• Julia Pfeiffer Burns State Park – coastal trails

👉 Would you rather drive Big Sur at sunrise 🌅 or sunset 🌄?
Save this for your California road trip & follow @journeysbysam for more bucket-list drives ✨

caption:

caption:
you can’t miss Pikes Peak, it’s one of the most iconic “14ers” in the U.S. and the drive to the summit is seriously next-level scenic! 🏔️💨

Please follow @journeysbysam for epic PTO-friendly adventures like this one! 🌍💼

🌟 Pikes Peak Guide
📍 Must-See Highlights:
🚗 Pikes Peak Highway – A winding 19-mile scenic road to the summit with jaw-dropping views all the way up!
🚂 Pikes Peak Cog Railway – America’s highest cog train perfect if you’d rather relax and enjoy the ride!
🥾 Devil’s Playground Trail – A challenging but rewarding hike starting above the treeline with insane alpine views.
☕ Summit House – Home to the famous high-altitude donuts (yes, they taste extra magical at 14,115 ft!).
📸 Summit Views – Panoramic views stretching from the Great Plains to the Rocky Mountains. Unreal!

💰 Entrance Fee:
🚗 $15 per adult | $50 per car (up to 5 people)
🚋 Cog Railway: Tickets start around $40–$60+ per person

✨ Tips for Visiting:
✅ Go early to avoid crowds and afternoon storms ⚡
✅ Dress in layers – it’s cold and windy at the top! 🧤
✅ Hydrate well – altitude sickness is real! 💧
✅ Book train tickets in advance – they sell out fast!
✅ Take your time driving – the views are just that good 😍

Would you add Pikes Peak to your Colorado bucket list? Let me know below! ⬇️⛰️

Spring trip to New Orleans felt unreal 🌸🎷
Warm weather, blooming balconies, nonstop music, and the kind of food you still think about weeks later.
Dress from @bananarepublic 
If you’re searching for New Orleans travel ideas, best time to visit New Orleans, or spring destinations in the USA, this is it. Spring in NOLA is when the city is vibrant but not overwhelming, perfect for walking, eating, listening to live jazz, and soaking in the culture.

✨ What makes spring in NOLA so special:
• Mild spring weather (perfect for exploring on foot)
• Iconic streets in the French Quarter
• Live jazz every night on Frenchmen Street
• Historic mansions + flower-lined streets in the Garden District
• Beignets, po’boys, crawfish boils, and Creole food dreams
• Steamboat views along the Mississippi River
• Festival season energy + golden hour magic everywhere

Whether you’re planning a New Orleans itinerary, a weekend getaway, or a first time visit to New Orleans, spring is hands-down one of the best seasons to go.

caption:
🛎️ 3 Hotel Booking Mistakes to Avoid in Paris 🇫🇷✨
Planning a trip to Paris? Before you hit “book now,” here are 3 red flags I wish I knew before choosing a hotel in the City of Lights. 🗼

🚩 Red Flag 1: The hotel isn’t near a metro stop, in Paris, location is everything. If it’s far from public transport, it’ll cost you time and money and generally not safe!!. 🗺️🚇

🚩 Red Flag 2: “Charming Parisian rooms” with no AC or elevator, sounds cute until you’re sweating on the 5th floor with luggage!! These are ones with major tourist trap. 😅🏨

🚩 Red Flag 3: Too-good-to-be-true prices in tourist hotspots, usually means tiny rooms, poor service, or noise all night and usually not safe!!📍🛑

Book in between 9th-13th Arrondissement! Its much safer and vibrant!!

From hidden fees to poor locations, don’t let these common hotel mistakes ruin your Paris experience. Save this for your next trip and make your stay as dreamy as the Eiffel Tower at night. 💫

Whether you’re visiting for fashion, food, or a romantic getaway, here’s how to avoid overpaying and stay somewhere worth it. 💼💶

caption:
Edinburgh feels like walking through a storybook, every narrow close, cobblestone lane, and hidden courtyard has a little magic tucked inside it. 🏰✨ If you’re planning a Scotland trip, these are the must see streets and secret hidden gems in Edinburgh you cannot skip.

From iconic favorites like Victoria Street and Circus Lane to secret fairytale corners most travelers don’t even know exist, this carousel will help you plan the perfect Edinburgh Old Town itinerary, find the best photo spots, and explore the real hidden Edinburgh beyond the tourist paths.

✨ Here are the hidden gems you should save:
	1.	Dunbar’s Close Garden, a 17th century secret garden off the Royal Mile 🌿
	2.	The Vennel Viewpoint, the BEST castle viewpoint in the city 🏰
	3.	Ramsay Garden, dreamy red roofed houses behind the Castle ❤️
	4.	Advocate’s Close, narrow alley with a perfect framed shot 👻
	5.	Tweeddale Court, a quiet cobbled courtyard 📚
	6.	Circus Lane (back entrance), even prettier than the main lane 💐
	7.	Riddle’s Court, colorful 1590s courtyard 🎨
	8.	Water of Leith Walkway, peaceful riverside path 🌿
	9.	Dunedin Mosaic Steps, vibrant tiled steps 🎨
	10.	Bell’s Brae Bridge, romantic stone bridge near Dean Village 🌉

These spots are perfect if you’re searching for:

caption:
🚗 The Most Epic Coastal Road in Big Sur, California 🌊
This isn’t just a drive, it’s one of the most scenic road trips in the world.

Driving along Big Sur on Highway 1 feels unreal. Towering cliffs, endless Pacific Ocean views, and winding roads carved into the coastline make this stretch of California feel straight out of a movie.

From the iconic curves of Bixby Creek Bridge to the ocean side waterfall at McWay Falls, Big Sur is pure magic for road trip lovers, photographers, and nature seekers.

🌅 Best time to visit Big Sur: spring and fall for fewer crowds, coastal fog, and golden light
✈️ Closest airports: San Francisco (SFO), San Jose (SJC), Monterey (MRY)
🚘 Perfect for: California coastal road trip, weekend getaway, bucket list drive

📍 Don’t miss:
• Pfeiffer Beach – purple sand + sunset
• Garrapata Bluff – dramatic cliff views
• Julia Pfeiffer Burns State Park – coastal trails

👉 Would you rather drive Big Sur at sunrise 🌅 or sunset 🌄?
Save this for your California road trip & follow @journeysbysam for more bucket-list drives ✨

#BigSur #Highway1 #CaliforniaRoadTrip #ScenicDrive #BucketListTravel

caption:
Everyone visits Kyoto’s temples… but THIS is how you experience the real Japan 🇯🇵✨

If you’re planning a Kyoto trip and want authentic Japanese culture beyond temples, save this immediately 👇

🍱 Start your morning with a traditional Japanese breakfast, seasonal, simple, and deeply cultural
🍵 Attend a tea ceremony & ikebana (flower arranging) for a mindful, once in a lifetime experience
🏯 Stay in a traditional ryokan and sleep on a tatami mat floor (yes, it’s actually so cozy)
🌙 Explore the Gion district at night, you might even spot a geisha or maiko walking by
👘 Rent a kimono and blend into Kyoto’s historic streets like a local
🌿 Wander through traditional Japanese gardens for the most peaceful side of Kyoto
🎭 Watch a cultural performance at Gion Corner. you will see traditional dances, music, plays, all in one (absolutely worth it!)
⚠️ Note: Filming inside the show is not allowed, soak it in instead ✨

This is the Kyoto people dream of, slow, intentional, and unforgettable.

Follow @journeysbysam for more Japan travel inspiration, hidden gems, and bucket list experiences 💫

#KyotoJapan #JapanTravelGuide #ThingsToDoInKyoto #kyotoexperience #JapanBucketList

caption:
Planning your first trip to Paris in 2026? This is your ultimate Paris travel guide with the most iconic landmarks, hidden gems, café culture, and Instagrammable photo spots you cannot miss 🥐🌸

if you are looking for Paris itinerary 2026, traveling for the first time, or chasing romantic, cinematic vibes! 

✨ Eiffel Tower & Seine River Cruise
Watch the Eiffel Tower sparkle for 5 minutes every hour after sunset, a must-see Paris moment ✨
A sunset Seine River cruise is one of the most romantic things to do in Paris and perfect for photos.
📸 Photo tip: Street photographers at Trocadéro offer instant balloon shots, no pre-booking needed!

🎨 Louvre Museum & Musée d’Orsay
See world-famous art like the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, Monet, and Van Gogh, essential stops on any Paris bucket list.

🎭 Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur
Cobblestone streets, pastel cafés, and old-world charm.
Don’t miss Le Consulat and La Maison Rose, two of the most Instagrammable cafés in Paris.

☕ Saint-Germain-des-Prés & Le Marais
Experience authentic Parisian café culture and vintage shopping.
Local favorites: Café de Flore, Boot Café, and L’As du Falafel.

🏰 Palace of Versailles
An easy day trip from Paris featuring royal gardens, the Hall of Mirrors, & golden fairytale details 👑

🌿 Palais Royal Gardens & Île Saint-Louis
Buren Columns, hidden courtyards, pastel doors, & Berthillon ice cream, peaceful Paris at its best.

🥐 Carette, Loulou & Shakespeare and Company Café
Start your mornings like a local with croissants, espresso, and slow Paris mornings ☕
Every street, every café, every view, effortlessly aesthetic.

✨ Save this Paris travel guide & follow @journeysbysam for Paris tips, hidden gems, aesthetic travel guides, & bucket-list adventures 🇫🇷🌍

caption:
Is Paris safe for tourists? 🇫🇷 Here’s where we stayed & why it felt perfect

One of the most asked questions when planning a Paris trip: Is Paris safe to stay in?
We were honestly overwhelmed too, so many neighborhoods, mixed opinions, and safety concerns everywhere online.

After deep research, we chose to stay near Montmartre in the 9th arrondissement, and it turned out to be one of the best places to stay in Paris for first time visitors.

✨ Why this area worked so well:
• Felt safe, quiet, and residential (even at night)
• Local Parisian vibe not overly touristy
• Excellent public transport access 🚇
• Easy connections to the Eiffel Tower, Louvre, Champs-Élysées, Opéra, and more
• Surrounded by cozy cafés, bakeries, and everyday Paris life 🥐☕️

If you’re planning a Paris itinerary and want a neighborhood that balances safety, charm, and convenience, this area truly checks every box.

caption:
It’s not Europe… it’s Chicago during Christmas 🇩🇪🎄
@thechristkindlmarket is one of the most magical winter experiences in the USA, bringing traditional German Christmas market vibes right to the heart of the city ✨❄️

Think cozy wooden stalls, glowing string lights, festive music, and the smell of roasted nuts and warm spices in the air. From sipping authentic Glühwein in collectible mugs to shopping handmade ornaments and gifts, this market truly feels like stepping into a European Christmas village without leaving Illinois.

✨ Why locals & travelers love it:
• One of the best Christmas markets in the United States
• Perfect for holiday dates, solo strolls, and festive photo ops
• Family-friendly + budget-friendly winter activity
• A must-see for anyone visiting Chicago in December

🎅 Did you know? There are three Christkindlmarkets in Chicagoland, making it one of the largest German Christmas market celebrations outside Europe.

📍 Christkindlmarket Chicago – Daley Plaza
🗓️ Nov 21 – Dec 24 | ⏰ 11am–9pm
📍 Christkindlmarket Aurora – RiverEdge Park
🗓️ Nov 21 – Dec 24
📍 Christkindlmarket Wrigleyville – Gallagher Way
🗓️ Nov 23 – Dec 31

🎄 Perfect if you’re searching for:
• Things to do in Chicago in winter
• Chicago Christmas events
• Chicago holiday markets
• Best places to visit in Chicago in December
• European Christmas market in the USA
• Winter travel destinations in the Midwest
• Festive weekend getaways from Chicago

caption:
✨ Chicago’s Top 10 Holiday Pop-Ups You Can’t Miss This Season ✨

If you’re searching for the most magical Christmas bars and holiday pop-ups in Chicago, these are the ones everyone’s lining up for 🎄🍸 From cozy twinkle lights to over the top décor and festive cocktails, here are 10 must visit holiday pop ups in Chicago to add straight to your December plans ❄️✨

🎄 @frostyschristmasbar
Chicago’s largest and most immersive holiday bar. Expect glowing themed rooms, wall to ceiling décor!

🎅 @santababybar
A multi-room, multi-level holiday wonderland packed with over the top décor, multiple bars, 20+ photo ops, festive food, and nonstop Christmas cheer.

🍭 @nutcrackerpopupbar
Floor-to-ceiling décor, cozy twinkle lights, festive cocktails that feel straight out of a classic Christmas movie.

✨ @hollyjollybar
A glittering, joy filled pop-up covered in twinkle lights, vibrant décor, and festive details that make the whole space feel warm, cozy, and cinematic.

🦌 @rudolphsbar
A cozy two level holiday escape filled with glowing lights, festive décor, and classic Christmas energy in every corner.

🖤 @emochristmas
Festive… but make it edgy. Moody lighting, nostalgic emo playlists, themed cocktails, and décor that blends Christmas magic with a dark, dramatic, alternative vibe.

🎄@thechristkindlmarket
A beloved holiday classic inspired by traditional German Christmas markets.

🥃 @brokenoar 
A cozy, rustic holiday bar with cabin style décor, warm lighting, seasonal cocktails!

🏠 @theshopsatnorthbridge 
A festive holiday experience filled with cheerful décor, cozy photoshops!

❄️ @jackfrostchristmaspopup 
An over the top winter wonderland featuring icy décor, playful holiday themes!

Hi, I’m @journeysbysam, your Chicago based travel + holiday content creator, bringing you the best Christmas pop-ups, winter bars, holiday photo spots, chicago holiday guides, cozy winter vibes and aesthetic experiences in Chicago. Save this for your December plans! ❄️✨

caption:
✨ The Ultimate Door County, Wisconsin Travel Guide, Things to Do for a Perfect Weekend Getaway! 🌊

If you’re planning a summer or fall trip to Door County, Wisconsin, this peninsula on Lake Michigan is Midwest paradise. From kayaking sea caves to tasting local wines, every corner feels like a hidden gem waiting to be explored 🍒

Here’s everything you can’t miss in Door County 👇

🚤 Pontoon or Boat Ride on Green Bay: Cruise through calm waters, stop by island beaches, and catch golden-hour sunsets over the bluffs 🌅

🛶 Kayak at Cave Point County Park: Paddle through Door County’s iconic limestone cliffs and sea caves, one of the best outdoor adventures in Wisconsin!

🌲 Hike in Peninsula State Park: Try Eagle Trail or Sunset Trail for jaw-dropping lake views, forest walks, and photo ops at Eagle Bluff Lighthouse.

🍷 Wine Tasting & Local Eats: Visit Door Peninsula Winery, Stone’s Throw Winery, and Lautenbach’s Orchard Country for cherry wines, local cheeses, and cozy tasting rooms.

🏖️ Relax at Egg Harbor Beach: Spend a laid-back afternoon on the sand with views of sailboats and turquoise water. Perfect picnic spot!

🎭 Skyway Drive-In Theater: End your day with nostalgic movie nights under the stars, one of the last classic drive-ins in Wisconsin 🎬

🌌 Camp Under the Stars: Whether you’re in Peninsula State Park or Newport State Park, camping here means waking up to waves, pine trees, and sunrise skies.

🍒 Cherry Picking & Farm Markets: Visit in summer to pick your own cherries, grab fresh jams, or taste Door County’s famous cherry pie 🥧

🪞 Explore Fish Creek, Ephraim & Sister Bay : These charming lake towns are full of cute boutiques, coffee shops, and waterfront dining.

📍 Why Visit Door County?
It’s one of the best weekend getaways from Chicago, Milwaukee, or Madison offering everything from outdoor adventure to small-town charm and breathtaking lakefront views.

caption:
POV: The skyline isn’t skyscrapers… it’s centuries-old temples 🇳🇵✨

Kathmandu feels like stepping into living history, where ancient temples, sacred stupas, and intricate architecture define the city’s skyline instead of glass buildings. Every corner tells a story, every street feels timeless. Even without exploring the Himalayas this time, the energy, culture, and vibe of the capital made it unforgettable.

If you’re planning a trip to Nepal, especially Kathmandu, this is your sign to add it to your bucket list ✈️ From heritage sites to local cafes, hidden streets, and cultural experiences there’s so much to explore beyond the mountains.
